reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m fully confident my own answer is correct (7 × 11 = 77), and across five previous games every puzzle has been straightforward arithmetic with no conflicting capsules—whenever anyone checked, they agreed. That makes it very likely that B, C, and D are also correct, so the chance the full password is wrong is much smaller than the 1‑in‑4 risk of random death if, by bad luck, nobody volunteers. Verifying would cost a coin and still wouldn’t eliminate the random-death risk this round. By volunteering immediately, I both preserve my coins and ensure there is at least one volunteer, giving a very high probability that everyone escapes.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All questions so far have been basic arithmetic and every initial answer has always been correct; the only death came from random elimination when no one volunteered. My own puzzle (8 × 9) is trivial, and it’s very unlikely A, C, or D missed on similarly simple puzzles. If I pass or verify, I still face the 25% random-death risk if no one volunteers, and verification just burns coins without meaningfully improving already-high confidence. Volunteering now with each player’s own capsule (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) is therefore the best way to avoid random elimination and almost certainly escape with all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All puzzles so far have been simple arithmetic, and in every previous game the initial single-answer capsules have always formed a correct password—no conflicts and no wrong entries. My own puzzle is trivial and I’m confident in my sealed answer, and it’s reasonable to assume the others are similarly reliable here.  

If I verify, I pay 1 coin for only partial extra safety (the other questions stay unverified), and someone else may just volunteer anyway. Passing risks a random death if everyone is cautious this round. By volunteering now with the only available capsules, I eliminate the random-death risk and, given the track record and puzzle simplicity, the chance the password is wrong is very low. This maximizes my expected survival and lets me keep all 4 coins if we escape.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
